name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: This is about making your vote count
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
This is what it comes down to: The sitting President of the United States is trying to exert direct control over the U.S. Postal Service to get around the Constitution, which says that states have control of elections. Courts are stopping him from directly suppressing mail-in ballots, so he's trying an end run around them using USPS.
Trump and the MAGA movement want to pick and choose whose vote counts. RUSH $35 or whatever you can right now to fight back against this abuse of the USPS and our fundamental right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050426_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
NYT: Trump Signs Order Seeking Federal Control of Mail Voting as He Promotes False Claims [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050426_3&amount=25]
Straight from the New York Times , Team:
" President Trump on [March 31] stepped up efforts to promote his false claims of widespread voting fraud, signing an executive order of questionable constitutionality seeking to create a national list of citizens that would determine voting eligibility and restrict mail ballots. "
Let's be clear: The president has no explicit Constitutional authority over elections, and much of this latest attack on our fundamental right to vote appears difficult to enforce and legally dubious.
This order directs the Department of Homeland Security to create a “ state citizenship list ” based on data from various federal databases. It also directs federal officials to send that made-up list to state election officials, and orders the attorney general to prioritize prosecution of election officials who provide ballots to voters not on the made-up list. It also directs the U.S. Postal Service not to transmit mail-in ballots from any individual not included on Trump's “ state citizenship list ," even though elections are mandated by the constitutional the be run by the states.
This order is yet another sign that the MAGA crew will do anything they can to codify their power permanently and silence the will of the people by suppressing and eliminating the votes we cast.
Our elections are sound and mail-in voting is secure, no matter what Trump says. Step up to defend our fundamental right to vote and the use of mail-in ballots with $35 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050426_3&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050426_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050426_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050426_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050426_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050426_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050426_3&amount=35]
We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, making it easier for voters to participate in our elections . States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. It's been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic.
But that doesn't stop Trump because he's absolutely terrified of what might happen in the next election.
Arizona’s Secretary of State (the official who runs elections), Adrian Fontes, reacted like this: “ The Constitution doesn’t allow the executive to take over elections administration, that’s a job for the state legislatures or Congress, and so I don’t think this is going to pass any sort of judicial muster. So this is a big, giant waste of time, and it’s an attention grab from the Trump administration. ”
He added: “ The greatest threat to American elections is Donald Trump lying about them. ” That''s because Donald Trump’s continued claims about large-scale “cheating” in elections are outright lies.
Protecting vote-by-mail for the upcoming midterms is crucial to make every voice heard in our elections. Will you chip in $35 or whatever you can to fight lies about mail-in voting and help make every vote count? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050426_3&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050426_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050426_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=SDWGDYKqW3XJY%2FzRWfXRuHadpSK1cKxp2rxBXknQjFv%2FPh74y%2FzvVDZ1yRmVkAKucTkHbZx1%2F8TJwgarKseSa8TH5S27slQGOyORI509nJ08ROHA6vGfobd1w7%2BSapU62IXjFNazOeBUpliA%2BbOgbM4qQtaEwsu%2BFxbfWeC83%2FylC%2FqFMuVuasCNGhadd5YEG3ibyuIHOiDJa9rZ%2FzmUS71bFEYCLYxi0LMhm2%2FD7C2x%2FThfqwL7Gd0azVvPwvCF6QLOJlRs7uQHlWhnjB%2FylIbjrnZXyTpIQrRgcHXxWaMoAP%2FrG6Vcke%2BDP5%2FDctQi4KG%2BlFvI6R1Vujzt5V7gbJZwt8d3wicwigWrcM3cD74HUgFf3tIO2nHq%2F%2B%2F3pGpQgaypW2i81HLwgDhu4i0etErwLdBZQNld8%2FwewNzlVo0%3D .

name: Royce White
subject: They Applauded a King
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE
body:
America bows to no monarch, no foreign power, and no corrupt political class. | [ ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=237e278ad2c9456eb510dc1bdbcf60c3&_e=4JoF-PtLxe0aBTReYdNYLJsEJ51Azu5RCWD_N0-ZSY7_OT1UFuBrZxea8BGm0WRQQeLB49G8bJIgnOogwiz6_r0IOGwdEMvmneYgBq05cPrcxTL8gVIZnDe9mGpl3P5A5drG0nH-Cq7TpoR6uA0glXQlo6KM0qWGd69Kq0FA3GW7Jf5zx4aFj40T1gLu2RUyv-P3zHJaljg1M7VguMTEpneB4h3peCR8dLYzoLKNfTNBxD11yDHH5GejwnUw3HKc-KT6KEm0HGZSJySZppiDBuKl7kYj39oYtEWx2fYQ801FALG4W1su6ks5H5Ulh7iEgPAEvqZvpVr6lIRl1VaQidXQSrbJMe20N0q6GyyKLJ26nTIdmz5TQpEzBh9Bp4nAlT_m-sskm9_gOOHACR1G5by8aCDqiLoqeglG4bzhkPMo1-Q-D8Wnuh8dTefWfJteBSdz_mb5BbKY4Z8NpEoh5JQfJyN36BuWMqOJJExWkkePyB5tj1EGwnQT1rOdopRBQxiZtDNjzGzKs9z-x9G3C3s8ejBJfJcmHdJ8jm-6EmS9dXByC-Vd-CCBD8Jz4U9R)
---

Patriot - The “No Kings” crowd just stood up and applauded a King.

You can’t make this up.

A foreign monarch walked into the United States Congress, referred to himself as “the sovereign,” and our elected officials grinned, clapped, and gave him the royal treatment.

That’s not public service. That’s submission.

**And the worst part? It wasn’t just one party. It was the whole UniParty machine on display.**

RINOs. Chuck Schumer. Amy Klobuchar. Republicans and Democrats standing together, applauding power, corruption, and foreign influence while pretending they work for you.

Let me be clear:

**There is only one King — Jesus Christ.**

America has no sovereign but the people.

No crown. No commonwealth. No ruling class.

**This is the United States of America, and our elected officials should be defending American citizens, not bowing before foreign elites.**

That’s why I’m running.

Because Washington is rotten. Because the UniParty is real. Because Minnesota deserves a fighter who will not bend the knee.

I’m not going to Congress to clap for kings.

**I’m going to fight for the American people.**

**[Chip in today and help me take this fight straight to the corrupt political class!](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=237e278ad2c9456eb510dc1bdbcf60c3&_e=4JoF-PtLxe0aBTReYdNYLJsEJ51Azu5RCWD_N0-ZSY7_OT1UFuBrZxea8BGm0WRQQeLB49G8bJIgnOogwiz6_r0IOGwdEMvmneYgBq05cPrcxTL8gVIZnDe9mGpl3P5A5drG0nH-Cq7TpoR6uA0glXQlo6KM0qWGd69Kq0FA3GW7Jf5zx4aFj40T1gLu2RUyc5gCC9QFCt2Hmtu9ZKmrUDJOXuLwRuP65uC6yKqGzNqp-yt3nOuAahC3T7MF5-QP9PWObgCC86IvHxbok_Dj_2Q4J4FBz1sF5SP9gN6able9cfRtF0unDQoNKRXvH681ftj7j7AJnHWlgyY8REbrKBalbwNo3edQ0e7QL7eKdgmNWYvJ-zrqSGMuLxrWgVZqpGuGGoirR2-xpB0HMYO7c2_B9vzNKgUCeLK54qBXcLuJ3zFubtBCLf8R9ECRLTxc1WmkfY8Nqkq2YxIUYfS9D7Db4Krlt_BCc2KN9_rkfkhoreFRib4nkSJswrl91OSZ1qwvpvb71lXjbRMrctz2R2roi-VJbJp6tooBYaXMQgXmOubzX9-qVMvpRzqQ3Ljz)**

They have the money. They have the media. They have the insiders.

We have the truth.

And the truth is still undefeated.

| [ **HOLD THE LINE** ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=237e278ad2c9456eb510dc1bdbcf60c3&_e=4JoF-PtLxe0aBTReYdNYLJsEJ51Azu5RCWD_N0-ZSY7_OT1UFuBrZxea8BGm0WRQQeLB49G8bJIgnOogwiz6_r0IOGwdEMvmneYgBq05cPrcxTL8gVIZnDe9mGpl3P5A5drG0nH-Cq7TpoR6uA0glXQlo6KM0qWGd69Kq0FA3GW7Jf5zx4aFj40T1gLu2RUyQ-6yhngNXAOti7p6sanUNjsfVarV_Vnskv_gCi8shoberBfIcFBXItcNBPiZf8EFwn3NfEfMEqJWjj90QQuD2s2PP_fWchEClkMT9fB2jadss2pay_1a9pD2pof-wZqdMyMoG9EawhL2JlIE9oyiwFlrQdnHPR2WnroKDqOswjTm4H8CX1YS4N9VIwEPFW6STn0IDaK8h3j4b5A5qLauzDkaIed8ZwHmu12XEGEPxS-_TIwBgaNHTf2waEzAClQyIothrLy03Q8bodUsvgi7Q3KtcTUoUzh7IoGoBa-Pxxo58T7iZrwlBDoi_Uj97bkM3eP-zQ9Ec36BT-eY6Jqne9O762E0ITU_2LLSQ23zQ3Y-1Y2xXgXQU2qIJ7vkppr1)

name: Nate Blouin For Utah
subject: Reaching the Final Stretch! 29 Days until ballots drop!
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://nateforutah.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c43df3fd60840df583f5d1de3&id=af2b50e2ce&e=44433285d9)
| | | | | | ,
---
| |
Happy Cinco de Mayo Eve! We’re down to 29 days until primary ballots start being mailed out, so every day counts. This is the most crucial time of the entire race, and we need your help to send a true progressive to Congress!
---
| | 📞 We’re holding phonebanks to reach out and get the word about about Nate’s campaign. Join us virtually **Tuesday, May 5th, at 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM** or **Thursday, May 7th at 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM** and let’s do this!
---
| [Phonebank For Nate!](https://nateforutah.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c43df3fd60840df583f5d1de3&id=79de9d7adf&e=44433285d9)
---
| |
**🕹️** Meet fellow supporters at **Quarters Arcade Bar Downtown (5 E 400 S)** on **Friday, May 8th from 7:30 PM to 9:30 PM.** Come learn more about getting involved and have some fun!
---
| [Come Hang Out!](https://nateforutah.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c43df3fd60840df583f5d1de3&id=a7d36b6039&e=44433285d9)
---
| |
**🚪** We’ll be holding a canvassing launch at **Fitts Park (3050 500 E)** on **Saturday, May 9th at 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM**. Come join to help get the word out about Nate!
---
| [Canvass For Nate!](https://nateforutah.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c43df3fd60840df583f5d1de3&id=26264ae1a6&e=44433285d9)
---
| |
Thank you so much to everyone who helped get us this far. This is the progressive campaign with the coalition to take on the establishment, powered by people, not corporate dollars. With your help, we can send a true progressive fighter to DC. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to reach out.

name: Wes Moore
subject: Leave no one behind
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| It’s Governor Wes Moore.

Five years ago, I had never run for political office. I was a former Army Captain, a dad to two young kids, and I ran one of the largest poverty-fighting organizations in the country.

But I knew more had to be done. While career politicians were saying that everything was great, **I was hearing from folks all across Maryland who were feeling left behind.** So I ran for Governor with a mission to make Maryland a state that serves all our people — and won. (And I’m not done yet!)

**Our mission must continue this year.** I’m running for re-election so we can keep building on the progress we’ve made on jobs, education, public safety, and more. There are tough fights on the horizon, and there’s so much more for us to accomplish together.

[I’m asking you to join our team by chipping in $5 or $10 right now, split between my campaign and the Voter Protection Project. Every dollar we raise helps defend this seat and build a campaign that’s ready for the challenges ahead.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108747?t=6&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa) If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Wes Moore and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108748?t=7&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108749?t=8&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108750?t=9&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108751?t=10&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108747?t=11&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
We’re making real progress — investing historic amounts in public education, bringing down costs for working families, and making our communities safer.

But we’re still in the federal government’s line of sight. Donald Trump has attacked me before, and he and his powerful GOP allies are going to do everything possible to flip our state back to red come November. This election could be an uphill battle.

**This powerful team has never backed down from a fight and I know you are not about to start now.** We have to hit the ground running, because our momentum now determines how we’re able to run the rest of this campaign.

[Please, split a $10 donation between my campaign and the Voter Protection Project today. Every donation helps us start the next phase of our campaign strong and fight back against Republicans’ advantage.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108747?t=12&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)

It’s time for us to come together again, across places and party lines, to prove to a dysfunctional Washington that there’s a better way forward.

Elevate,

Wes
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108747?t=13&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)
878179
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=24197%2E878179%2EyXJZoa)

name: Rep. Gloria Johnson
subject: Stand up against the shameful GOP power grab
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF GLORIA JOHNSON, LISA SORENSEN, TREASURER
body:
We need to call this special session what it is: a white power rally.

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/ec05bffd610c/stand-up-against-the-shameful-gop-power-grab?e=dc5f639d62)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
|
---
| |
Friends,
You’ve probably seen the news: Governor Bill Lee is rushing state legislators to a special session on Tuesday (tomorrow), at the direction of his boss Donald Trump, in order to redraw our state’s political districts. They’re trying to steal Tennessee’s only Democratic-held Congressional seat, and ensure that Republicans don’t ever have to compete with Democrats at all for federal representation.
**We need to call this special session what it is: a white power rally.** By breaking up Tennessee’s 9th Congressional District, Republicans are silencing the voices of Black voters, who make up a majority of Shelby County and 60% of the district. Generations of brave Americans - including countless Tennesseans - fought for equal rights and representation and fought against Jim Crow-era codified racism. Now, Bill Lee and Marsha Blackburn want to undo that progress and drag us back to the darkest corners of our history.
[**TAKE ACTION HERE**](https://us.list-manage.com/x4N2LLr1N8T?e=dc5f639d62&c2id=395895520870fa96cf2587ee59cac4d0)
**This is a shameful power grab** , and I’m sickened by our governor and the bootlicking Republican supermajority. Perhaps they should review our own Tennessee law (TN Code § 2-16-102) that says “The districts may not be changed between apportionments.”
They don’t care that Tennessee is [ranked dead last for K-12 education spending](https://us.list-manage.com/13CXkQkuINC?e=dc5f639d62&c2id=395895520870fa96cf2587ee59cac4d0) or that farms are going bankrupt, rural hospitals are at risk of closing, and prices are skyrocketing... they only care about clinging to power and appeasing their DC bosses.
**But we are not powerless in this moment**. We need to stand tall, stand together, and STOP these extremists before the damage is done. You can show up and speak out in the coming days to let Bill Lee and Marsha Blackburn know that **Tennessee is a lot more than just MAGA bootlickers** , and we deserve representation too. You can add your name to this petition today to say “Tennessee Is Home”:
[**SIGN THE PETITION**](https://us.list-manage.com/lcbo_xoiQZF?e=dc5f639d62&c2id=395895520870fa96cf2587ee59cac4d0)
And finally, I will not be able to take donations once the special session starts so if you can, please donate tonight to help us fight back against this power grab.
[**DONATE HERE**](https://us.list-manage.com/t4_dt2zjeyP?e=dc5f639d62&c2id=395895520870fa96cf2587ee59cac4d0)
I’ll be in touch with you again this week, so please stay tuned and be prepared to take any action you can to stop this reckless power grab.
In solidarity,Gloria JohnsonState RepresentativeTennessee House District 90

---
| |

---
| | PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF GLORIA JOHNSON, LISA SORENSEN, TREASURER
---

name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: 3 Weeks Out - Republicans Undecided
body:
Plus: Iran's regime is cracking | New episode for dpwillis67@gmail.com

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://us.list-manage.com/1CxBH3N40LX?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)
| | | | | |
Three weeks until New Mexicans pick a Republican nominee for governor. The new [Albuquerque Journal poll just dropped](https://us.list-manage.com/ST7GHSdsscv?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e) — and it tells you everything about how wide open this thing still is.

Then we get to Iran. The regime is cracking. And what the Wall Street Journal just reported that they're considering...well, you have to hear it to believe it!
---
[|
---](https://us.list-manage.com/_Xst7P0AJqE?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)
| |
🏛️ **HULL LEADS AT 30% — 40% STILL HAVEN'T DECIDED**
Gregg Hull is in front. Doug Turner is at 21%. Duke Rodriguez is stuck in single digits.
But the number that actually matters is 40% — that's how many Republican and Independent voters still haven't picked a candidate. Three weeks out.
We break down why nobody has been able to lay a glove on Hull, and the one move Doug Turner has to make in the next 14 days — or this race is over.

🔥 **THE ATTACK MAILERS HIT — AND ONE OF THEM ACTUALLY LANDS**
Duke Rodriguez voted in Arizona in 2024. And 2022. And 2018. And 2016.
A PAC mailer just made sure every Republican primary voter knows it.
Meanwhile, Duke's people are firing back at Doug Turner over $6 million in state contracts Turner's firm allegedly took from Michelle Lujan Grisham to push her COVID agenda.
We watch both candidates' ads on the show. One is professional. The other looks — well, you'll see. And we tell you which attack insiders say is actually working.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://us.list-manage.com/7K00f9F4Wmn?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)
---
| |
🐬 **IRAN'S REGIME IS CRACKING — AND THEY'RE PLOTTING KAMIKAZE DOLPHINS**
70% inflation. Hundreds of millions in losses every day. No oil moving to China. The Iranian regime is desperate.
How desperate? The Wall Street Journal just reported they're contemplating strapping mines to dolphins to go after U.S. ships.
That's not a joke. That's actually being floated.
Mark walks through why Trump's blockade is working better than the media wants to admit — and why Scott Jennings completely lost his cool on CNN this week. We're not defending Jennings. But you have to see what triggered it.

✈️ **WARREN AND BUTTIGIEG JUST TOOK DOWN SPIRIT AIRLINES**
Spirit went bankrupt Saturday. 30 regional airports lost service overnight.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent named names on Sunday morning TV: Elizabeth Warren wrote the letter that blocked the JetBlue merger. Pete Buttigieg's DOT killed the deal.
Why does it matter to you in Albuquerque? Half the Sunport's traffic flies Southwest. And when Spirit's discount flyers disappear from connecting hubs, your fares are about to go up across the board. Mark explains exactly how this hits your wallet.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://us.list-manage.com/ufgcHdCWoRU?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)
---
| |

PLUS: A Hollywood reality star is running for LA Mayor against Karen Bass — and his ad, filmed standing in an Airstream parked on his burned-out home, is one of the most effective candidate spots we've seen all year. We also break down the new study showing millennial dads have **tripled** the time they spend with their kids since the 1960s.
---
|
---
| | ✅ **Watch** on[ YouTube](https://us.list-manage.com/CbkFiHzC5hw?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)

✅ **SUBSCRIBE** to [our channel](https://us.list-manage.com/nh3Iv_lSUz8?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e) and hit the 🔔 for updates

✅ **RATE & REVIEW** us on [your favorite podcast platform](https://us.list-manage.com/1AapdUr1U8r?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)

✅ **DONATE** to support independent, fearless commentary at: 🌐 [NoDoubtAboutItPodcast.com](https://us.list-manage.com/J50d3PNeZrg?e=567664f14c&c2id=f551a60afe6194f7ea2dcf92821ad62e)

Thank you for being part of this community — real talk, real issues, and no spin.

**Mark & Krysty Ronchetti **
_No Doubt About It Podcast 🎙️_

name: Eileen Laubacher
subject: problem
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eileen for Colorado
body:
2026 is our best chance ever to defeat Lauren Boebert. But right now, we have a problem – and I need your help.

Hello,
2026 is our best chance ever to defeat Lauren Boebert. But right now, we have a problem – and I need your help.
**Our next fundraising deadline is at midnight tonight, but we’re still falling short of our critical $50,000 goal and what we need to send Lauren Boebert packing.** [**So please, will you make a monthly donation right now to help us get back on track to defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats this November?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130750824/618804823/156431970?refcode=20260504pm-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjlmNmM0NzUtMTc0OC1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=M6b6jjvgsJ28MYdsc3-dBR95Ux-a5bM1kitG6eEJPS8=)

name: RoyCooper.com (via Cooper Victory Fund)
subject: Emergency Action: Safeguard fair representation and voting rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Take a stand in defense of voting rights and equal representation for all
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6532?source=&t=1&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
| | | |
---
[](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/voting-rights-0526?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=email_su_pros_jtk416&t=8&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
Peter – the President Trump-stacked Supreme Court just gutted Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, which bans racially discriminatory electoral laws and maps.
**_[If that’s all you need to hear, please take 60 seconds right now to sign on to Roy’s emergency petition in support of fair representation and voting rights before 11:59 p.m.](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/voting-rights-0526?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=email_su_pros_jtk416&t=9&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)_**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/voting-rights-0526?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=email_su_pros_jtk416&t=10&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
---
**By effectively empowering GOP-held state legislatures to silence millions of voters of color – _and millions of North Carolinians_ – ahead of the midterms, the _Louisiana v. Callais_ ruling is an egregious attack on the better part of a century’s worth of progress. It also risks giving MAGA Republicans all the seats they need to lock in control of the House for decades to come.**
The Supreme Court has joined President Trump and his congressional allies in chipping away at the very idea that in our democracy, every vote matters equally.

However, this appalling decision only underscores what Roy has said before: **Democracy is not something guaranteed to us – it’s something we must fight for. **
**_[That’s why we’re counting on at least 50,000 folks like you to join the fight alongside him in support of fair representation and voting rights ASAP. Will you please take 60 seconds now to add your name to our emergency petition before the deadline at midnight?](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/voting-rights-0526?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=email_su_pros_jtk416&t=11&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)_**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/voting-rights-0526?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=email_su_pros_jtk416&t=12&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
---
We can’t afford to go back.

– Team Cooper
| |
* * *
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8188?t=2&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**_[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6533?source=&t=3&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)_**
| | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6297?source=&t=4&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
---
| | [DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6298?source=&t=5&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
---
| | [SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=6&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
---
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=7&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina.
---

Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.
_

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)
_If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, _[submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=13&akid=1288%2E124072%2EAJKcWI)_

even more resources into defense while families here at home have to decide between buying groceries and keeping the lights on. _Our national budget signals our country’s priorities to the rest of the world._ It should reflect what the American people want: investments in our communities, protecting access to healthcare, strengthening infrastructure, and making sure working families aren’t left behind. **But having that voice in Congress—and the ability to fight for those priorities—depends on the strength of this campaign.** The truth is: showing up strong now is how we prepare to push back against misplaced priorities later. clean_body: Donald Trump and his allies are pushing for a massive increase in military spending | | | Friend, Donald Trump and his allies are pushing for a massive increase in military spending—a record-shattering—pouring even more resources into defense while families here at home have to decide between buying groceries and keeping the lights on. Our national budget signals our country’s priorities to the rest of the world. It should reflect what the American people want: investments in our communities, protecting access to healthcare, strengthening infrastructure, and making sure working families aren’t left behind. But having that voice in Congress—and the ability to fight for those priorities—depends on the strength of this campaign. The truth is: showing up strong now is how we prepare to push back against misplaced priorities later. Each of these individual reasons is why we have specifically chosen YOU for this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity. Take 5 minutes NOW and complete the questionnaire. Your responses are CRUCIAL to the future of MAGA. We're giving you until MIDNIGHT tonight. | | We need your response. Please don't delay. For America, Operation Red Strike | | Paid for by Operation Red Strike, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Hudson for Congress and NRCC. 320 First St SE Washington, DC 20003 | This email is sent to %%Emailc%% by Operation Red Strike. name: Team WFP
subject: The Dem establishment is putting their thumb on the scale
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
They don't want the stronger candidate, they want one who will bend to corporate donors. | | | Peter, Just like they did in Maine, the Democratic establishment is trying to put their thumb on the scale in another primary — not to support the stronger candidate, but the candidate who will bend to their corporate donors. **The DCCC just announced they are going to try to derail WFP champion Randy Villegas in his race for Congress in California’s 22nd Congressional district.** Because they know he’s not the type of candidate to blindly push their preferred agenda — instead, he’ll fight for working families day in and day out. And corporate donors can’t allow that. Randy has all the momentum in this race right now, and the Democratic establishment is panicking. He is the top Democratic fundraiser in the race, despite not taking a penny from corporate interests. The son of Mexican immigrants, **he would also be the first Latino representative that CA-22 — which is 73% Latino — has ever had.** In a district on the front lines of defending immigrant neighbors from ICE, the establishment is backing Jasmeet Bains, who refused to vote on a bill that requires ICE agents to lower their masks. [With his primary less than a month away, we need to show up right now for Randy and fend off these attacks. Please rush a split contribution today between WFP and Randy Villegas’ campaign for Congress in California.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9MP9mRKAo8bh79eCgcpM_SxI3l_xEnlSFn3yt-fFNYnuRX4Yj0N_9i2GJgiptZYZeuB3QZIaUoYdn0oAuYrsPcXcgHPnqLr--CVzylDKh8DqyCDWlWWZDCPvgbPTp6bKRDQqBJdhVsWkp1WbGloDwMJwRn0Qj-s21-TDtlEXQN9eu5uJZWbir092iQ5aPqG6j3bdnKffafNQ6fax-wsHnqEzT90Pgm0QEyN0nnuqItROtvbaAYfBoDDkwdR75T5GZh9qJ3YeEmmCivC5KDhuM3fFs0lR-FaJL-AMQ9J1kw9y_mTYnDLmvfartBz1ztbWo5dK3MTdinINZVVk_HBp2EUsEZH2g7YUQMqQNwlfK3F5-HUOSk1YxexNKb_4ZECbmZKGmxxCPEFbCVBCCi8DuHA/4qd/biLmImKBSU-FYRnQynmJHg/h0/h001.ctShj-WHQNRrSOTr_5jJ5tJl-ELYElnLE0sS8lG1ROQ) **[Donate $10 »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9MP9mRKAo8bh79eCgcpM_SxI3l_xEnlSFn3yt-fFNYnuRX4Yj0N_9i2GJgiptZYZe-B-GWgAWQPO0MkQ6NOfq89oiybbFHNeOprf5oxyY0tgUBlGRrsS_chwkUOlj2Op6xkBhEle8xxPn1BqnLO5w8bDxNvqao79TV8Ah2Hi9H6K7tXou5LV5KKsQVvMVulvPayfdUbewPtbeQBriKjTVyNSQnvQhxremyadQ39M3mcMURy2v-0AzL8zOXG2EAK5fhwbp9BxQYp5AM1eh6AAaDBJpdjfGdw0Gfu-C7dG2JRalybKL_9AmsDQoxYewJPjhIryXuL7Gq2yT7E0lxv-R4FHhVkJCWM-OPljf9EnkHUMw8wGntb_ucmw1JxkWkU28IEs42RAZQm3200MdnxtnkA/4qd/biLmImKBSU-FYRnQynmJHg/h1/h001.t6mUvz7vJ1B7NVSwLN9VQrI47Rc3My_QR6lXQQ1Xk3k)** This will be one of the most competitive races of 2026, and Randy is the obvious choice for working families in California. He’s racked up more key endorsements than his opponent, including Sen. Bernie Sanders, Dolores Huerta, the Congressional Progressive Caucus, the National Education Association, the United Autoworkers, and National Nurses United. Meanwhile, Jasmeet Bains has raked in money from corporate donors — many of whom have donated to the district’s Republican incumbent in the past. **Bains was also the only Democrat in the Assembly to vote against Prop 50, Newsom's Election Rigging Response Act.** Randy Villegas is the ONLY candidate in this race with the energy and momentum to defeat incumbent Republican David Valadao in November. But first, we need to overcome these attacks by the corporate Democratic establishment. clean_body: They don't want the stronger candidate, they want one who will bend to corporate donors. | | | Peter, Just like they did in Maine, the Democratic establishment is trying to put their thumb on the scale in another primary — not to support the stronger candidate, but the candidate who will bend to their corporate donors. The DCCC just announced they are going to try to derail WFP champion Randy Villegas in his race for Congress in California’s 22nd Congressional district. Because they know he’s not the type of candidate to blindly push their preferred agenda — instead, he’ll fight for working families day in and day out. And corporate donors can’t allow that. Randy has all the momentum in this race right now, and the Democratic establishment is panicking. He is the top Democratic fundraiser in the race, despite not taking a penny from corporate interests. The son of Mexican immigrants, he would also be the first Latino representative that CA-22 — which is 73% Latino — has ever had. In a district on the front lines of defending immigrant neighbors from ICE, the establishment is backing Jasmeet Bains, who refused to vote on a bill that requires ICE agents to lower their masks. This will be one of the most competitive races of 2026, and Randy is the obvious choice for working families in California. He’s racked up more key endorsements than his opponent, including Sen. Bernie Sanders, Dolores Huerta, the Congressional Progressive Caucus, the National Education Association, the United Autoworkers, and National Nurses United. Meanwhile, Jasmeet Bains has raked in money from corporate donors — many of whom have donated to the district’s Republican incumbent in the past. Bains was also the only Democrat in the Assembly to vote against Prop 50, Newsom's Election Rigging Response Act. Randy Villegas is the ONLY candidate in this race with the energy and momentum to defeat incumbent Republican David Valadao in November. But first, we need to overcome these attacks by the corporate Democratic establishment. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . Because of your support in April, we’re gaining ground and bringing real energy to this race.

clean_body: | | | | | "Trump's core supporters turn on him,” Fox News Peter, Fox News is saying YOU quit on President Trump. Is that true? I didn’t think I’d ever have to ask you that question, but I need an honest answer right now. Rush back your “I STAND WITH TRUMP” survey NOW >>> | | The media pollsters are blasting out headline after headline claiming Trump’s most loyal supporters have walked away. TV... social media... the front page of morning newspapers... It’s everywhere, and I’m worried sick. These polls are driving a MASSIVE EDGE through the heart of the Republican Party – threatening to FRACTURE the entire America First movement from the ground up. So I’m coming to YOU for some clarity and support. Are you still 100% behind President Trump? | | Your response is critical. Adriana Guzmán Fralick here. My Republican colleagues told me YOU were someone who would give it to me straight. I’m running for Attorney General because President Trump needs fighters by his side – not backstabbers, not RINOs, and not fair-weather friends who fold under media pressure. Today, I’m testing whether these so-called “pollsters” are right. Is this the end of MAGA? Have YOU abandoned President Trump? Last year, Republicans like you delivered the BIGGEST POLITICAL COMEBACK in American history. Now I need to know if you still have the willpower and guts toFIGHT. Because the pollsters say you don’t... and we only have a limited amount of time before their lies are permanently engraved in the minds of MILLIONS of American voters. That’s why I’m sending you your official “I STAND WITH TRUMP” survey. I need your responses sent back to me by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT. No excuses. We CANNOT let the media run wild with the lie that Trump’s base is vanishing. If we let that narrative take root, it could spell DISASTER for Republicans in 2026. I’m all-in for President Trump — every minute, every breath, every ounce offight I’ve got. Now I’m putting YOU to the test. Will you rush back your “I STAND WITH TRUMP” survey by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT? I’ll be waiting eagerly for your response. For Country, Adriana Guzmán Fralick Republican for Attorney General (NV) | | | | Paid for by Adriana for Nevada | | P.O. Box 370801, Las Vegas, NV, 89137 | | |

They backed the only Democrat in the California Assembly to vote against Newsom's Election Rigging Response Act, Prop 50, to fight Trump's gerrymandering scheme. They backed a candidate who has taken hundreds of thousands from Big Pharma and insurance companies. They backed a candidate who refused to stand up to ICE while masked agents kidnap families off streets in our 70%+ Latino district. But on the ground, we’re winning. Civil rights leader Dolores Huerta is behind us. All four in-district Democratic County Chairs have endorsed our campaign. Dozens more local leaders are doing everything they can to help us win this race. The community is choosing us. Washington is choosing politics as usual. The establishment has failed working families again and again by force-feeding us candidates with no positive vision for how they're going to improve our lives. They take PAC money, run hollow campaigns, and wonder why working people tune out. That's exactly what's happening here, and the Central Valley deserves better. DC insiders are trying to rip away our right to choose our own candidate. This is the time to take a stand. **TOP NEWS**
**" Exclusive: Texas teacher accused of molesting student was on 'do not hire' list"** via [San Antonio Express-News](https://substack.com/redirect/628f21b5-b9d4-4257-9c2a-5b5b766e484e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)' **Bayliss Wagner** - Youths walk by the windows of the dining hall on their way to lunch in 2006 at the Giddings State School in Giddings, Texas. When one of their teachers was arrested and accused of repeatedly molesting an eighth-grade student last fall, Elgin school district officials expressed shock.

"Accusations such as these are concerning, and if proven to be true, unacceptable," the Austin-area district told KVUE in November, noting they had moved quickly to assist police when the claims were made against **Turran Fennix**.

But it wasn't the first time that Fennix, 34, had been accused of having inappropriate interactions with a child, according to records obtained by Hearst Newspapers. Two years earlier, Texas' juvenile justice agency barred Fennix from future work in the system after he was caught giving a skin-to-skin back massage to a student in a dorm room, a clear policy violation. Administrators determined he should be fired for the incident, but allowed him to resign instead. Elgin Middle School hired him just a few months later.

Fennix's previously unreported trajectory from the Texas Juvenile Justice Department to a middle school raises questions about how thoroughly Elgin vetted the employee and whether the state disclosed his full background. _([SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/628f21b5-b9d4-4257-9c2a-5b5b766e484e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" As the state targets cities over ICE policies, Austin considers next steps"** via [TPR](https://substack.com/redirect/b9e1f541-f5c5-4a8d-8a81-3d9547697b27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Mose Buchele** - In some ways, Texas has provided a crystal ball for the rest of the country when it comes to immigration policy. Before the Trump administration poured billions into immigration enforcement and built partnerships between local police and state and federal immigration agents, Texas was already doing it. So, for those opposed to the deportation surge, Texas is worth paying attention to.

That was one of the takeaways from a panel Saturday at the KUT Festival. "**Kristin Etter** , the director of policy and legal services at the Texas Immigration Law Council, said, "Here in Texas, we have kind of a four- to five-year head start when it comes to the rest of the country. We have operated a program called Operation Lone Star that has used state resources. So we have a model here in Texas for this police/ICE collaboration."

Another takeaway from the panel: In a state where much of the voting public and all state political leaders support aggressive immigration enforcement, opposition becomes local. In liberal Austin, local leaders have tried to craft policies that would limit police partnering with ICE, but the governor took notice and the Austin Police Department had to soften one policy under threat of funding cuts.

City Council Member **Zo Qadri** said Austin still has tools at its disposal "to make sure that folks are protected and are safe." City Council Member **Jos e Velasquez** pointed to millions in city funding for local immigration advocacy groups and the council's support for Austin's "safe to call" initiative as examples. _([TPR](https://substack.com/redirect/b9e1f541-f5c5-4a8d-8a81-3d9547697b27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Tornado in North Texas Destroys Several Buildings and Displaces Residents"** via [The New York Times](https://substack.com/redirect/1ba47b22-3ceb-4d59-a70b-898010932a6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Jesus Jim enez **- The whir of chain saws and the beeping of utility trucks were the most prominent sounds around Mineral Wells, Texas, on Wednesday afternoon, after a tornado the previous night ripped through a section of the city.

In one industrial sector of Mineral Wells, a town of about 15,000 residents west of the Dallas-Fort Worth area, roofs had been sheared off buildings and other structures had been reduced to piles of what looked like matchsticks. But the destruction appeared limited to an area of commercial properties that were largely empty.

Most residential homes in the area were unscathed, and no deaths were reported, though at least five people were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries and many residents were displaced, city officials said.

**Christopher Hester** , 33, said that he was sitting in his living room with his wife on Tuesday afternoon when their front door suddenly burst open. He said they immediately took cover in the hallway of their home. _([The New York Times](https://substack.com/redirect/1ba47b22-3ceb-4d59-a70b-898010932a6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" High gas prices eating into Texans' budgets, fueling inflation"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/2530c8ae-cb49-499f-9038-da088809b741?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Paul Cobler** and**Colleen DeGuzman** - The large signs outside of gas stations across Texas intended to catch drivers' eyes are lately doing so for all the wrong reasons.

At a Valero gas station in Edinburg, **Reynaldo Zarazoa** , who installs tile for a living and has to haul tools and equipment around in his gray GMC pickup, watched Thursday as the display on the gas pump reached $78 -- nearly a fifth of his weekly income.

"My work is cheap and the gas is expensive," said Zarazoa, a father of two living in Peñitas, a small city in the Rio Grande Valley. "But I have to drive every day, every day for work."

The price of fuel has risen significantly since the U.S.-Israel war on Iran began on Feb. 28, closing a key international shipping lane for oil and gas, the Strait of Hormuz. The average price for a gallon of gas in Texas was $2.55 in early February, according to AAA. It had risen to $3.78 on Wednesday morning. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/2530c8ae-cb49-499f-9038-da088809b741?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Court sides with 'EPIC City' developers over state agency in fair housing fight"** via [Dallas Morning News](https://substack.com/redirect/07443e71-f930-448a-9d7d-de7dd85d2189?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Nick Wooten** - "This ruling confirms what we have maintained from the beginning -- that Community Capital Partners has been willing, ready, and committed to following Texas law at every step," said Imran Chaudhary pictured above at the EPIC City project site outside of Josephine.

A Travis County judge has ordered a state agency to comply with the fair housing agreement it made with the developer of a Muslim-centric neighborhood in North Texas.

The Texas Workforce Commission must abide by the terms of a September 2025 settlement it reached with developer Community Capital Partners. The group is behind The Meadow, previously called EPIC City.

The 201st District Court issued the injunction Tuesday. A judge also denied the state's request to dismiss the case on Wednesday, allowing Community Capital Partners' lawsuit to move forward. _([DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/07443e71-f930-448a-9d7d-de7dd85d2189?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_
* * *
**2026**
**" Republican Brett Ligon, former Montgomery County DA, wins special election for Texas Senate seat"** via Texas Tribune's**Alejandro Serrano** and **Colleen DeGuzman** -**Brett Ligon,** the Republican former district attorney of Montgomery County, won a special election Saturday for the state Senate district vacated by **Brandon Creighton** , who was tapped last year to be chancellor of the Texas Tech University System.

Ligon defeated Democrat **Ron Angeletti** , an educator and small-business owner who was the only other candidate on the ballot. The two are set for a rematch in November for a full four-year term. In the meantime, Ligon will serve the remainder of Creighton's term, which runs until the start of the next legislative session in January.

"The voters of SD4 have delivered a clear message tonight. Conservative values and Republican dominance in Texas are alive and well," he said in a statement declaring victory 25 minutes after polls closed. "Democrats from Texas and all over the country threw everything they had at us. Democrat politicians were here today in full force, campaigning hard for my opponent. As if we had been flying the 'Come and Take It' flag - they tried - and they failed."

Senate District 4 - which spans across Chambers, Harris, Jefferson, and Montgomery counties - voted for President **Donald Trump** by a 34-point margin in 2024, making it one of the reddest seats in the upper chamber. It is considered a virtual lock for Republicans in November, even with midterm political headwinds that have left Democrats optimistic about flipping GOP legislative seats. _([Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/b9efb17d-e6c1-4ad7-9766-faaf08ceb3be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" 'It's a mess': With no Trump endorsement in Texas Senate race, Cornyn and Paxton are locked in an expensive brawl"** via [CNN](https://substack.com/redirect/b3854ffd-8a6a-4d70-969c-a58d981fad89?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Patrick Svitek** - Nearly two months ago, President **Donald Trump** promised he would make an endorsement "soon" in the Republican primary runoff for US Senate in Texas. That endorsement still has not arrived -- and its absence is keeping the race highly competitive.

Three people involved in the race said they no longer expected Trump to get involved in the battle between Sen. **John Cornyn** and Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton**. However, they cautioned he could change his mind before the May 26 runoff, keeping both sides on edge in the final weeks.

While Democrats haven't won statewide in Texas since 1994, they have a Senate nominee in state Rep. **James Talarico** who is energizing liberals ahead of the November midterms and raising massive sums. Republicans long wanted to avoid a damaging runoff that could make things easier for Talarico, in part by having Trump boost one candidate and pressure the other to back down.

Trump was likely to eventually endorse Cornyn after he and Paxton advanced from a March 3 primary, sources told CNN at the time. However, Trump grew frustrated after Republican operatives leaked his expected plans, the sources said. Trump also fielded calls from some of his most ardent supporters, pushing him not to endorse Cornyn and warning it would be a mistake with his "Make America Great Again" base. _([CNN](https://substack.com/redirect/b3854ffd-8a6a-4d70-969c-a58d981fad89?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Talarico leads Cornyn, Paxton in Senate matchups, UT poll finds"** via [Houston Chronicle](https://substack.com/redirect/b79d2526-cad0-40d0-a914-9805b86d0cda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Benjamin Wermund** - **James Talarico** , a Texas Democratic primary candidate for U.S. Senate, leads both Republicans vying to face him in the November U.S. Senate contest, according to a new poll released Wednesday.

Talarico led U.S. Sen. **John Cornyn** 40% to 33% in a potential matchup, and held a 42% to 34% advantage over Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** , the University of Texas at Austin's Texas Politics Project survey found. In both cases, 19% of voters said they were still undecided.

The results come as Paxton and Cornyn remain locked in a bitter primary runoff, and the pollsters said Talarico's lead is likely due, at least partially, to the ongoing GOP battle. No Democrat has won a statewide election in Texas in three decades.

"While the Democratic candidate leading two established Republicans in these match-ups is sure to raise some eyebrows," they wrote, the results "reveal a Republican electorate still registering the effects of the months-long, bruising, negative campaigning by Paxton and Cornyn, and the GOP divisions the race has exacerbated." _([HOU CHRON](https://substack.com/redirect/b79d2526-cad0-40d0-a914-9805b86d0cda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" TX-18 Democratic primary runoff: Christian Menefee vs. Al Green"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/5bba6904-85c6-4ab7-886e-df1b8215cc6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Gabby Birenbaum** - In this rare clash of sitting incumbents, Houston Democratic Reps. **Al Green** and **Christian Menefee** are squaring off after the Legislature consolidated their two Houston districts, both long represented by Black Democrats, into one during last summer's redistricting.

The runoff pits the Texas delegation's newest member, Menefee, 38, against one of its longest-tenured -- the 78-year-old Green. With the two candidates largely aligned on policy, the race has turned on each of their fighting styles and whether voters prefer Green's congressional experience and protests against **Donald Trump** or Menefee's millennial energy and history of waging legal battles with Texas Republicans.

The two do have differing stances on cryptocurrency, with Green a skeptic and Menefee more willing to embrace the industry as it spends lavishly in support of his candidacy.

Texas' 18th Congressional District has a majority Black voter population and is one of the deepest blue seats in Texas. Long a bastion of Black political power -- past representatives include **Barbara Jordan** , **Craig Washington** and **Sheila Jackson Lee** -- it was left without representation for nearly a year after Rep. **Sylvester Turner** died in office in March 2025. Menefee led the November special election and won the January runoff to finish out Turner's term. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/5bba6904-85c6-4ab7-886e-df1b8215cc6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**STATE GOVERNMENT**
**" Dan Patrick eyes closing 'gambling loophole' for prediction markets. The feds stand in Texas' way."** via Texas Tribune's Ayden Runnels - In March, Lt. Gov.**Dan Patrick** directed state senators to explore ways to close "gambling loopholes" that allow online prediction markets to operate in Texas, raising concerns that state elections and sporting events could be manipulated for profit.

Patrick's directive was the first time a state leader officially acknowledged the existence of rapidly growing prediction markets, which let users wager on outcomes tied to anything from the weather to election winners and sports scores.

Any attempts by the Texas Legislature to restrict prediction operators, however, would run into federal roadblocks as the Trump administration insists that oversight belongs to a U.S. agency, not the states.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has taken vigorous steps to retain exclusive regulatory oversight, suing to block five states from taking legal action against predictive markets, including a lawsuit filed Tuesday against Wisconsin. _([KHOU](https://substack.com/redirect/4063e233-e93b-49b2-b6f3-fa529dbef831?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Future of Texas hemp uncertain in THC fight: 'We're all still very anxious and nervous'"** via [KXAN Austin](https://substack.com/redirect/d645e685-76f2-4a3e-8e89-6de9111a76a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Adam Schwager** - The immediate future of the Texas Hemp industry likely rests in the hands of Travis County District Court Judge **Daniella Deseta Lyttle**. The hemp industry has asked Judge Lyttle to grant a temporary injunction against the enforcement of new Texas Department of State Health Services (DSHS) rules -- which they claim will shutter the industry. The rules first went into effect on March 31, but were later halted by a temporary restraining order which expires on May 1.

If Judge Lyttle grants the Texas hemp industry the injunction? "We'll expand on our THC product line. We're actually having a conversation about having a third location if this goes through," Happy Cactus co-owner **Todd Harris** said. "We're all still very anxious and nervous."

If the order is denied it would result in skyrocketing licensure fees and the ban of intoxicating hemp flower, which Harris says makes up 70% of his sales. And instead of opening a new store, he might have to close one. "This [east Austin] location and how it survives and thrives is very much tied to how the injunction and what happens in that courtroom," Harris said.

In the courtroom, the Texas Hemp industry called two witnesses to the stand: Galveston-county based hemp store operator **Kenneth Burner** , and Texas Hemp Business Council (THBC) Executive Director **Mark Bordas**. Burner, an Iraq veteran, highlighted his practice of employing those who've either served in the military or as a first responder. "Tells us something about their character right from the beginning," he said. _([KXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/d645e685-76f2-4a3e-8e89-6de9111a76a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Commissioners accept financing deal for new baseball stadium that gives the county a park"** via [San Antonio Report](https://substack.com/redirect/18761323-d68a-4413-ad55-5d08209c576d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Shari Biediger** - The decisions made during negotiations between Bexar County Commissioners and Missions baseball owners have been mostly behind closed doors -- until now.

On Tuesday during Commissioner's Court, the officials agreed to a kind of trade with Designated Bidders, revealing something of how that deal will be structured.

The agreement approved by commissioners gives the county two parcels of land for a park near the San Pedro Creek Cultural Park in exchange for restructuring the financing agreement it made with the team's ownership group in 2024 to build a new downtown ballpark.

At the same time, county officials agreed to release its portion of the tax increment guarantee from the Houston Street Tax Increment Reinvestment Zone, which is estimated to make up 14% of the total ballpark project cost. _([SA REPORT](https://substack.com/redirect/18761323-d68a-4413-ad55-5d08209c576d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**LOCAL GOVERNMENT**
**" Powerful North Texas transportation leader fired after 35 years on the job"** via [WFAA](https://substack.com/redirect/3e3da131-0725-4c34-aa18-fa191faa8b51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Rachel Behrndt** \-- **Michael Morris** , a longtime regional official who oversaw hundreds of billions of dollars of transportation projects, was fired from his position after 35 years on the job, officials said.

The firing followed a months-long fight and a weeks-long court battle that pit two powerful government entities against each other over the firing and has now led TxDOT to propose a new agreement clarifying which group has the power to terminate the official, according to documents reviewed by WFAA.

Morris served as the Director of Transportation for the North Central Texas Council of Governments (NCTCOG) for 35 years, according to a statement from the organization. His last day on the job was April 28, 2026, the statement reads.

"We are grateful for his more than 40 years of service to the region, including 35 years of leadership that helped shape one of the nation's most dynamic transportation systems," the statement reads. _([WFAA](https://substack.com/redirect/3e3da131-0725-4c34-aa18-fa191faa8b51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Dallas' Highland Park votes to leave Texas' second-largest public transit system"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/b14709f7-502e-4221-873f-f1c87beb7584?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Joshua Fechter** and **Colleen DeGuzman** -- Dallas Area Rapid Transit, the state's second largest public transit agency, fractured Saturday as voters in one suburb chose to leave the transit system.

**Highland Park** will pull out of DART, according to election returns Saturday. It's the first time any city has pulled up its stake in the agency since 1989 when Flower Mound and Coppell opted to leave.

Voters in Addison and University Park also weighed in on proposals to leave the system but chose to remain in the agency. This leaves 12 cities connected through DART.

Nearly 70% of voters in **Highland Park** decided to discontinue DART in the town, according to final but unofficial returns. The result was mirrored in Addison, where 70% voted to remain connected to the system; University Park was more narrowly divided, with 54% voting to stay. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/b14709f7-502e-4221-873f-f1c87beb7584?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" After Austin train derailment, calls grow to move freight rail out of downtown"** via [Austin American-Statesman](https://substack.com/redirect/3a1dc472-f6e1-40c0-b525-f9f97afc2bf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Alex Driggars** - A freight train derailment in downtown Austin last month is prompting Travis County Judge**Andy Browwn** to renew calls to move a major rail line out of the city's urban core -- a long-debated idea that has yet to gain traction.

Seven Union Pacific cars derailed early on April 23 as a 230-car train rounded a sharp curve near West Third and Bowie streets in the Seaholm District. No one was injured, but the incident snarled traffic for hours and delayed school bus routes in West Austin as crews worked to clear the scene.

The train was transporting "mixed commodities" rather than hazardous chemicals, but Brown -- a longtime passenger rail advocate who oversees emergency management in his role as county judge -- said the cars could just as easily have been carrying something far more dangerous, like chlorine.

"Chlorine can cause a cloud of chlorine gas, and that would be a horrible thing," Brown said. "Just the fact that you're potentially carrying hazardous chemicals right through this huge metro area with condos literally feet away from where this curve was is not great." _([AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/3a1dc472-f6e1-40c0-b525-f9f97afc2bf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Is APD really 'quiet quitting'? Critics raise concerns as response times lag"** via [Austin American-Statesman](https://substack.com/redirect/929d5837-38a4-475b-9d60-09f69bdebd3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Austin Sanders** and **Karina Kumar** - Last month, Austinites and officials alike heaped praise on the Austin Police Department after officers responded to a mass shooting on West Sixth Street in 57 seconds and took down the gunman minutes later.

The praise for the speedy response was rare for the Austin Police Department, which has struggled for years with slow response times and regularly faces online criticism and complaints. The rapid intervention also raised a question: Is the Police Department getting faster?

An American-Statesman analysis of median response times over the past decade shows that the swift reaction to the Buford's Bar shooting was an outlier. Since 2017, response times - while seeing some improvement in the last year under Police Chief **Lisa Davis** - have lagged even as 911 call volumes have decreased.

The findings are reflected in public complaints. Since the city's police oversight agency began categorizing grievances in 2022, the most common type has been "no assistance," which includes slow responses and alleged no-shows. _([AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/929d5837-38a4-475b-9d60-09f69bdebd3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" A solar surge in Austin: New projects target reliability and climate goals"** via [Austin American-Statesman](https://substack.com/redirect/e7f409ea-2ac7-404b-b42f-84ffaf79afc3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Chaya Tong** - Austin is betting on solar power as it confronts a growing energy crunch, approving a slate of projects aimed at boosting local generation and cutting reliance on the broader ERCOT grid.

The City Council recently approved three proposals designed to expand that local capacity: a new solar facility on a closed city landfill, solar installations across city facilities and negotiations for up to 40 megawatts of battery storage in partnership with local manufacturer Base Power, Inc.

Austin Energy spokesperson **Matt Mitchell** said the initiatives would add about 78 megawatts of capacity -- enough energy to power nearly 20,000 homes for a year -- and the agreement with Base Power offers comparable value to the 100 megawatt Jupiter battery contract the council approved last July.

Mitchell said the utility was already 73% carbon-free last year, offsetting 65% of customer demand, and argued that expanding local generation helps stabilize costs by reducing the need to import power during extreme weather and peak demand. _([AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/e7f409ea-2ac7-404b-b42f-84ffaf79afc3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" San Antonio psychiatric hospital laying off nearly 650 employees"** via [San Antonio Express-News](https://substack.com/redirect/8a976a01-2ee3-4dde-ae0d-ec02bf53e8e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Lily O 'Neill **- Laurel Ridge Treatment Center on the far North Side plans to lay off 648 employees starting in June, after the federal government terminated its Medicare and Medicaid coverage.

After losing its Medicare and Medicaid funding, San Antonio's Laurel Ridge Treatment Center is planning to lay off hundreds of employees.

"As a result of this regulatory action, we must implement a workforce reduction," **Ashley Sacriste** , CEO of Laurel Ridge, said in a statement.

The hospital is "deeply disappointed by this outcome and recognizes the significant impact it will have on our dedicated team members and the communities we serve," she added. _([SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/8a976a01-2ee3-4dde-ae0d-ec02bf53e8e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" A Houston ISD exec tapped for Beaumont schools superintendent during its second state takeover"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/629d2a15-05af-404f-bfed-f770103cc44a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Sneha Dey** - Texas officials again tapped a Houston schools administrator to helm a state takeover, this time for Beaumont ISD.

**Sandi Massey** is the new state-appointed superintendent of Beaumont ISD as the district has struggled with years of poor academic performance. Massey comes from the nearby Houston ISD, where she was the chief of schools during a takeover that has been controversial among parents but lauded among state leaders.

"I know what it takes to transform a district," Massey said at a press conference Wednesday. "I am ready to do it here in Beaumont and make this city the best instruction you've ever seen."

Massey is the third Houston schools official tapped in recent weeks to help shepherd a state takeover. Texas Education Agency commissioner **Mike Morath** appointed **Ena Meyers** , who was a Houston ISD chief, as superintendent of Lake Worth ISD last week. And **Peter Licata** , on his first day on the job as the state-appointed superintendent of Fort Worth ISD, hired another Houston ISD chief **Daniel Soliz** as his deputy. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/629d2a15-05af-404f-bfed-f770103cc44a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**BUSINESS NEWS**
**" D-FW's GameStop bids $56 billion to buy iconic internet auctioneer eBay"** via [Dallas Morning News](https://substack.com/redirect/d8ff4fec-f4dc-4bb2-8b84-6488b1b3210c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Brian Womack** - GameStop is proposing to acquire eBay in a deal that would bring one of the bigger names in Internet history under a North Texas-based company.

The Grapevine-based retailer said it submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire the Silicon Valley e-commerce company for $125 per share, according to a GameStop statement. The aggregate undiluted equity value is approximately $55.5 billion.

The proposed offer is comprised of 50% cash and 50% GameStop common stock. The North Texas company has built a 5% economic stake in eBay through derivatives and beneficial ownership of stock.

**GameStop** -- known in some circles more for its "meme stock" days and a push into Bitcoin -- could be making a significant move with the proposed acquisition. The company, which has been shuttering stores where it sells video games, consoles and collectibles, saw its sales decline in the previous fiscal year though it swung to an operating income from a loss. _([DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/d8ff4fec-f4dc-4bb2-8b84-6488b1b3210c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

Thank you for reading as a FREE subscriber. » Become a PAID subscriber for $7/mo or $70/yr and SAVE TIME and be BETTER INFORMED. A WORD FROM SENATOR PHIL GRAMM ABOUT MUST READ TEXAS " If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: .
