### Tonight, We Watch the Results
Again, I'll be going live around 7:45 after the polls close to watch the results come in. You can watch at **[theangrydem.com](https://substack.com/redirect/63360f36-bb36-4127-9fff-6d39d6a5ca60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[theangryohioan.com](https://substack.com/redirect/dedaada8-2d0a-47d5-9432-6249a0f32d37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , or on **[Facebook](https://substack.com/redirect/842b44cd-479e-4293-be28-cd3584d03537?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , **[X,](https://substack.com/redirect/28c5a9ca-88de-483d-928a-a1f04d21ab2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[YouTube.](https://substack.com/redirect/8c54683b-3cfd-4807-a095-678b152964ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

clean_body: It's Election Day, everybody! | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? We need to find other ANGRY DEMOCRATS. Please consider sharing and subscribing to fuel the ANGER for change. | | | May 5 | | | | | | | | | | | | It's Election Day, everybody! I hope everyone has a plan to go vote today. Tonight, I'll be going live around 7:45 after the polls close. You can watch at , , or on , , and . I'm just going to be hanging out, watching the results come in, giving my comments, and maybe having a few guests swing by as the night unfolds. Below, I'll include some resources that may help you before you head to the polls. Google your county name and "Board of Elections." Your local board of elections usually has everything you need, including polling locations, sample ballots, candidate lists, and basic voting information. And please, take a few minutes to look at your sample ballot before you go. Know who is actually running in these races. Do not just walk in and let the first name you recognize make the decision for you. There are candidate lists available, and is another great resource. It can show you polling locations, sample ballots, candidate information , and what you need to bring with you to vote. It may be raining, so take an umbrella. The best times to go are usually outside the rush hours. Avoid before work, lunch, and right after work if you can. If you go during one of the random slower parts of the day, you will probably be in and out. And honestly, this is a midterm primary, so it should not be anything like waiting in line during a presidential year. The Statewide Races I'm Watching The first race I'm watching is the Republican primary for governor. Vivek Ramaswamy is the endorsed GOP candidate, but Casey Putsch , also known online as has been getting a lot of support on social media. He has not raised a ton of money, so the question is whether that online energy actually translates into votes. I do not think the real question is whether Vivek wins. I think the real question is how much he wins by. That margin will tell us something about how real the online grassroots energy around Casey actually was, and the actual popularity of Vivek. The attorney general race between and . John is the endorsed Democratic candidate, and Elliot has some baggage. I do not know if everybody knows the actual stories behind Elliot Forhan's baggage, but to be perfectly honest, it seems like some of it was overblown and turned into a smear campaign. He definitely has people who strongly support him, but I do not know if that will be enough to overcome the Democratic endorsement. See some Elliot news below The Angry Ohioan I met Matt a few months ago. He reached out after his social media feed had been serving up some of my POWW! Protect Ohio Women at Work videos. I knew Matt's name because I had heard good things about him as a candidate. We did a fun interview talking about my 13 years as legal counsel to the House Democrats and how it ended with Allison unceremoniously... 2 days ago 5 likes Sarah Cherry Then there is the Secretary of State race , which is probably the one everyone is paying attention to statewide. running against . Russo is the establishment Democrat, the House Minority Leader, and basically the party's candidate. Hambley is the grassroots candidate. He has built a strong campaign, raised a hell of a lot of money, and created real energy around his race. If Brian Hambley wins, it will be considered an upset, but also somehow expected. I know that sounds contradictory, but that is exactly how this race feels. If Allison Russo pulls it off, that will also feel natural because she is the party-backed candidate. But it would also feel a little unnatural because Hambley has clearly built something real. See my interview with the candidates below The Angry Ohioan I recently sat down with former State House Minority Leader Allison Russo and Bryan Hambley. Both are Democrats running for Secretary of State here in Ohio... 2 months ago 7 likes 1 comment The Angry Democrat Northern Ohio Congressional Races Obviously, I'm watching Ohio's 7th District. There are eight candidates in this race, and they are going to be splitting up votes like a Thanksgiving turkey. , I can see this race going a lot of different ways. There are definitely candidates people consider favorites, but I do not know how far that goes when you have this many people dividing the field. This could be a blowout, or it could be a total toss-up where almost anyone can win. See my interviews and write ups below | | | | ---|---|--- | Mar 25 | I'm also watching Ohio 's 9th Congressional District Republican primary, which is fascinating. The incumbent is the Iron Babushka (tm ) herself, . But Republicans are throwing a lot at this seat because they think she may be vulnerable. Josh Williams, Derek Merrin, Alea Nadeem, Madison Sheahan, and Charles Kinsel are all in the mix. And they are raising serious money. Williams has raised around $800,000. Merrin has raised around $760,000. Nadeem has raised around $700,000. They are fighting like hell over there because they think this is their shot. To be perfectly honest, I would not bet against Marcy Kaptur no matter what the political landscape looks like or how much money Republicans raise. Derek Merrin may be the favorite to win the primary, but Josh Williams has raised more money, and Nadeem could possibly slide in if the others split votes. Who knows, maybe one of the other candidates catches just enough support to squeak through. It is almost like Ohio's 7th District, but for Republicans. ? Ohio 's 14th District is another one I'm paying attention to. On the Republican side, Nicole Frenchko is to primary David Joyce. It ain't gonna happen, but she is out there. On the Democratic side, , , and are competing for the nomination. What surprised me most is how little money has been raised in that race. Maria is the top fundraiser with only about $21,000. Carl has around $13,000. Bill O'Neill did not raise anything. I do not know if the plan was just to win the primary without fundraising, but considering the year Democrats may have, I expected a lot more money to go into that race. See my interviews below The Angry Ohioan Hey everyone, Matt Diemer here, The Angry Democrat... a month ago 3 likes The Angry Democrat The Local Races That I am Looking Forward To Moving into the local races, there are a few statehouse races I'm watching closely. House District 18 is one of them. Gabe Crenshaw, Samuel Gruber, Peter Hoffman, and Davida Russell are all competing for the Democratic nomination. That is a race worth watching because these local primaries are where the future of the party actually gets shaped, whether people want to admit it or not. House District 19 is another big one. Dionna Gore, Cheryl Perez, and Nicole Sigurdson are all competing for the Democratic nomination. Nicole is the endorsed candidate in that race, but I would not count out Dionna or Cheryl. Nicole has been carrying some baggage that I do not think she has fully shaken, and that has rallied a lot of people against her. There is also a Democratic write-in candidate in House District 19, Azaadjeet Singh. To be perfectly honest, I did not know about him until he texted me a couple days ago, but I wish him well. State Senate District 21 is also interesting to me. Ken Smith, the incumbent, is facing Dolores Gray Ford. I watched their League of Women Voters forum, and you can read my write-up in the link below. It should go without saying that Ken Smith, being the incumbent, is also the endorsed candidate. Read my LWV roundup below The Angry Ohioan Does a Good Communicator Make a Good Legislator... 24 days ago 2 likes 1 comment The Angry Democrat County Council Races to Watch Cuyahoga County Council Districts 3 and 11. In County Council District 3, incumbent Martin J. Sweeney is facing Anise Mayo. I do not think this is going to be much of a competition. I expect Sweeney to run away with it. But trust me, I love surprises. County Council District 11 is much more interesting. Shirley Smith, Ebony Spano, and Christine McIntosh are all running for the seat, and there was no endorsement in that race. I have met each of them on multiple occasions, and I think this is going to be a very interesting race to watch once the results start coming in. Tonight, We Watch the Results Again, I'll be going live around 7:45 after the polls close to watch the results come in. You can watch at , , or on , and Make a plan. Look up your polling location. Check your sample ballot. Know who is running. Bring an umbrella if it is raining. Vote. And, Stay Angry. Join otherANGRY DEMOCRATS** by support Matt's work. Subscribe and Share! | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Matt Diemer Berea, Ohio ? ?

name: The PreBorn! Team
subject: An excellent way to honor a mom this Mother’s Day
body:
But not every woman will experience Mother’s Day that way.

| | | | [](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGK3DAMhp9GuWVQ7Ik9OeSwNAQKTaEstMegtbUZQ2wH25Ptvn3JkD7A3iT9P_-HpD_TPJFbXVh-kufe7G5OeabAfw2vK4fyQZ8l3mOIiXz0s0ChamxrbA9fU9neWCVFxX2jte60RrxV9942eNVEVqKw8ialUfatfTeoqBMor6Zy_ZGELbYNSkR9MbbD7p2FYcPNrWO44mOzVDhfTAyZ007F7exCiEcVw8VEX639vZQtg3wBMYIYF7fzZUv8FtPTAGI8OxCjj-XOKdeWPkGO2YAcpuEFW6Fep9cfIBT5AnIQNxBqySAHCiDUo_g5x0cyDHLYUswbm-LCckqerXt4kAN7cus5NOQ3cksAOZzQg3lqMRQOB2hq5l-6_vb7e_3_FlXq7fbh1tVlpeGKy5H5XLR8_TV7L_4FAAD__yZYnSg)
---
Friend of Life, This Mother’s Day weekend, many people will call their moms, sit around a table together, or take a moment to remember the woman who gave them life. In those moments, it becomes clear just how much of who we are has been shaped by someone who chose to step into motherhood, even when it required sacrifice, patience, and strength. **But not every woman will experience Mother’s Day that way.** Right now, there are women sitting alone, trying to decide if they will become mothers at all. Everything around them is telling them to move quickly, to decide before they have time to process what’s happening, before they have space to ask questions, before they have ever seen the life they carry. Some of them are sitting in their cars, staring at the doors in front of them, telling themselves this is the only way forward, even though something inside them keeps asking them to wait just a little longer. **[And if nothing interrupts that moment, the decision will be made in minutes.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGK3DAMhp9GuWVQ7Ik9OeSwNAQKTaEstMegtbUZQ2wH25Ptvn3JkD7A3iT9P_-HpD_TPJFbXVh-kufe7G5OeabAfw2vK4fyQZ8l3mOIiXz0s0ChamxrbA9fU9neWCVFxX2jte60RrxV9942eNVEVqKw8ialUfatfTeoqBMor6Zy_ZGELbYNSkR9MbbD7p2FYcPNrWO44mOzVDhfTAyZ007F7exCiEcVw8VEX639vZQtg3wBMYIYF7fzZUv8FtPTAGI8OxCjj-XOKdeWPkGO2YAcpuEFW6Fep9cfIBT5AnIQNxBqySAHCiDUo_g5x0cyDHLYUswbm-LCckqerXt4kAN7cus5NOQ3cksAOZzQg3lqMRQOB2hq5l-6_vb7e_3_FlXq7fbh1tVlpeGKy5H5XLR8_TV7L_4FAAD__yZYnSg)** That is why PreBorn! exists. Because when a woman is given the chance to see her baby on an ultrasound, something begins to change. The screen lights up. The heartbeat fills the room. What once felt distant suddenly becomes real in a way she cannot ignore. For the first time, she is no longer choosing in fear or pressure, but with clarity. And more than 80% of women choose life in that moment. Months later, she is not carrying the weight of a decision she cannot undo or wondering what might have been. She is holding her baby. **[This Mother’s Day, there is no more meaningful way to honor a mom in your life than by helping another woman become one.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGK3DAMhp9GuWVQ7Ik9OeSwNAQKTaEstMegtbUZQ2wH25Ptvn3JkD7A3iT9P_-HpD_TPJFbXVh-kufe7G5OeabAfw2vK4fyQZ8l3mOIiXz0s0ChamxrbA9fU9neWCVFxX2jte60RrxV9942eNVEVqKw8ialUfatfTeoqBMor6Zy_ZGELbYNSkR9MbbD7p2FYcPNrWO44mOzVDhfTAyZ007F7exCiEcVw8VEX639vZQtg3wBMYIYF7fzZUv8FtPTAGI8OxCjj-XOKdeWPkGO2YAcpuEFW6Fep9cfIBT5AnIQNxBqySAHCiDUo_g5x0cyDHLYUswbm-LCckqerXt4kAN7cus5NOQ3cksAOZzQg3lqMRQOB2hq5l-6_vb7e_3_FlXq7fbh1tVlpeGKy5H5XLR8_TV7L_4FAAD__yZYnSg)** $28 provides one ultrasound, and that single moment can change everything. Will you step into that moment today?
---
[**Give Now**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGK3DAMhp9GuWVQ7Ik9OeSwNAQKTaEstMegtbUZQ2wH25Ptvn3JkD7A3iT9P_-HpD_TPJFbXVh-kufe7G5OeabAfw2vK4fyQZ8l3mOIiXz0s0ChamxrbA9fU9neWCVFxX2jte60RrxV9942eNVEVqKw8ialUfatfTeoqBMor6Zy_ZGELbYNSkR9MbbD7p2FYcPNrWO44mOzVDhfTAyZ007F7exCiEcVw8VEX639vZQtg3wBMYIYF7fzZUv8FtPTAGI8OxCjj-XOKdeWPkGO2YAcpuEFW6Fep9cfIBT5AnIQNxBqySAHCiDUo_g5x0cyDHLYUswbm-LCckqerXt4kAN7cus5NOQ3cksAOZzQg3lqMRQOB2hq5l-6_vb7e_3_FlXq7fbh1tVlpeGKy5H5XLR8_TV7L_4FAAD__yZYnSg)
---
Grateful for you,

The PreBorn! Team
